records.ety: [L. expurgatus, p. p. of expurgare to purge, purify; ex out, from + purgare to cleanse, purify, purge. See Purge, and cf. Spurge.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Expurgated (ks`pr*gā"td); p. pr. & vb. n. Expurgating (ks`pr*gā"tng).]

defs: To purify; to clear from anything noxious, offensive, or erroneous; to cleanse; to purge; as, to expurgate a book.
